research.interface: S3 REST API 2006-03-01 multipart upload operations via AWS SDK for Python (boto3) 1.43.50

official_sources.title: CompleteMultipartUpload - Amazon Simple Storage Service
official_sources.used_for: completion envelope of PartNumber+ETag per part, parts concatenated in ascending part-number order, special errors EntityTooSmall/InvalidPart/InvalidPartOrder (400) and NoSuchUpload (404), multipart-object ETag ending in -<part count>, and checksum fields in the response

official_sources.title: Uploading and copying objects using multipart upload in Amazon S3 - Amazon Simple Storage Service
official_sources.used_for: 100 MB best-practice threshold for switching to multipart, part numbers 1-10,000, recording PartNumber+ETag from each UploadPart response for completion, composite checksums requiring consecutive part numbers starting at 1, and the requirement to complete or abort so stored parts stop accruing charges

official_sources.title: upload_part - Boto3 documentation
official_sources.used_for: boto3 S3 client parameter names (Body, Bucket, Key, PartNumber, UploadId, ChecksumAlgorithm) and the ETag/ChecksumCRC32 response fields the uploader must surface

official_sources.title: Retries - Boto3 documentation
official_sources.used_for: retryable error codes in the SDK's standard retry mode (RequestTimeout, SlowDown, Throttling, ThrottlingException, transient 5xx) and total_max_attempts semantics used by the harness to disable SDK-internal retries

research.language_rationale: boto3 is AWS's official Python SDK and the credible S3 integration surface in Python; the uploader drives an injected boto3 S3 client, and the acceptance tests pin the wire contract with botocore's Stubber, which validates each request against the real S3 service model.

verified_facts: CompleteMultipartUpload concatenates parts in ascending part-number order and requires the PartNumber and ETag returned by each UploadPart; an out-of-order list fails with InvalidPartOrder (HTTP 400).
Special completion errors: EntityTooSmall (a non-final part under 5 MB), InvalidPart (part missing or ETag mismatch), NoSuchUpload (upload ID invalid, aborted, or already completed; HTTP 404).
The ETag of a completed multipart object is not an MD5 of the object data and ends in -<number of parts>.
Part numbers may range from 1 to 10,000; when additional checksums are used, part numbers must be consecutive and start at 1, so a deterministic uploader numbers parts 1..N.
AWS recommends multipart upload for objects of 100 MB and larger; each part except the last must be at least 5 MB.
After initiating a multipart upload you must either complete or abort it; only then does S3 free the stored parts and stop billing for them (AbortMultipartUpload returns HTTP 204).

boto3's standard retry mode treats RequestTimeout, SlowDown, Throttling, ThrottlingException and transient 5xx responses as retryable; configuring retries={'total_max_attempts': 1} disables SDK-internal retries so the caller owns the policy.
boto3 upload_part takes Body/Bucket/Key/PartNumber/UploadId (plus ChecksumAlgorithm on the multipart create) and returns ETag and per-part checksum fields such as ChecksumCRC32.
